# Section index pages vs. auto-generated indexes â€” design

*Status: design (brainstormed 2026-06-17). Scope: docs site only â€” no knock code, schema, or
architecture change.*

## Problem

The Docusaurus docs site (`website/`, sidebar fully `autogenerated` from the `docs/` tree) has
hand-written section landing pages in two of its five DiĂ¡taxis sections:

- `docs/how-to/index.md` â€” a manually-curated bullet list of the how-to guides.
- `docs/explanation/index.md` â€” a manually-curated bullet list of the explanation pages.

`tutorials/` and `reference/` have **no** index; `examples/` links a hand-written catalogue
(`examples/README.md`).

Each manual index **duplicates the auto-generated sidebar**: it re-lists, by hand, the pages that
already live in the folder. That list is a second source of truth that silently drifts â€” add,
rename, or remove a page and the index must be hand-edited to match, with nothing enforcing it.
This is the same class of drift the project already designs out elsewhere (the generated
`docs/reference/` with its CI drift gate; `Field(description=...)` as the single source for schema
*and* docs).

**Goal:** remove the drift risk from the two manual section indexes, structurally â€” not by adding a
checker, but by making the index *derive* from the pages.

**Non-goals:** uniformity for its own sake (we deliberately do **not** add indexes to `reference/`
or `tutorials/`, which gain nothing from a landing page); reworking `examples/README.md` (a genuine
narrative catalogue, not a bare index); any change to knock code, the policy/config schema, the C4
model, or the ADR set.

## Decision

Convert `how-to/` and `explanation/` to Docusaurus **`generated-index`** category pages, and move
the per-page editorial content (short label, one-line blurb, reading order) into **front-matter on
each page** â€” the single source of truth, co-located with the page it describes.

The discovery that drove the shape: the child pages currently carry **no front-matter** (they open
with a bare `# H1`). So the curated short labels and one-line blurbs in the index pages exist
*nowhere else* â€” a naive switch to `generated-index` would list pages by their long raw `# H1`
with no blurb. The fix is to give that editorial content a home in each page's front-matter, where
it travels with the page across renames and moves.

## Changes

### 1. Per-page front-matter convention (`how-to/*.md`, `explanation/*.md`)

Each content page gains:

```yaml
---
title: <short curated title>        # e.g. "Attach a scan result" â€” the index's bold label
description: <one-line blurb>        # the sentence currently in the index list
sidebar_position: <n>               # preserve today's curated reading order
---
```

â€¦and the redundant body `# H1` is **removed** (Docusaurus renders the page heading from
front-matter `title`; keeping the H1 would double it). `title` then drives the sidebar entry, the
`generated-index` card heading, the browser tab, and the meta description (an SEO bonus). Command
names (`knock attach`, etc.) already appear in the blurbs, so dropping the longer H1 loses nothing.

*Rejected alternative (minimal):* keep the `# H1`, add only `sidebar_label` + `description`. The
`generated-index` card would then show the long raw H1 (e.g. "Attaching a scan result
(`knock attach`)") instead of the short curated label. Rejected in favour of short, consistent
titles everywhere.

### 2. `_category_.json` â€” the index becomes generated

For `how-to/` and `explanation/`, replace the link with a `generated-index` that carries the
section's intro prose in `description` (rendered as markdown):

```json
{
  "label": "How-to guides",
  "position": 2,
  "collapsed": true,
  "link": {
    "type": "generated-index",
    "slug": "/how-to",
    "title": "How-to guides",
    "description": "Task-oriented guides â€” each solves one concrete problem against a running knock. â€¦ More task walkthroughs live alongside the runnable example policies."
  }
}
```

The page **list** is now derived from the folder + each page's front-matter â€” no hand-maintained
list remains.

**`explanation/` carries extra content that `generated-index` will not auto-list.** Its current
index mixes three local sibling docs (`deletion-and-retention`, `attestations`, `sbom`) with three
**external** GitHub links (`architecture/design.md`, `roadmap.md`, the ADR directory â€” all
`exclude`d from the site, so they are GitHub URLs, not in-site docs) and a "Why knock" pointer.

A `generated-index` description is rendered as **plain text** (`<p>{description}</p>` in
`DocCategoryGeneratedIndexPage` â€” verified against the installed `@docusaurus/theme-classic`), so
markdown links placed there would **not** be clickable. The three external pointers therefore move
to the **site footer** (empty today â€” `links: []`), where they become reachable from every page
rather than from this one landing â€” strictly better discoverability. The category `description`
keeps only plain-text intro prose; the three local pages are covered by the auto-generated card
list. "Why knock" is the site landing page (`/`) and needs no extra pointer.

### 3. Navbar (`website/docusaurus.config.ts`)

The two navbar items link the deleted docs by `docId`; repoint them at the new `generated-index`
slugs:

```diff
- {type: 'doc', docId: 'how-to/index', label: 'How-to', position: 'left'},
+ {to: '/how-to', label: 'How-to', position: 'left'},
- {type: 'doc', docId: 'explanation/index', label: 'Explanation', position: 'left'},
+ {to: '/explanation', label: 'Explanation', position: 'left'},
```

### 4. Footer (`website/docusaurus.config.ts`)

Populate the empty footer with the three deep-dive pointers displaced from `explanation/index.md`,
so they stay reachable site-wide: **Architecture & design**, **Roadmap & product thesis**,
**Decision records** (all GitHub URLs), plus the existing **GitHub** repo link.

### 5. Deletions

Remove `docs/how-to/index.md` and `docs/explanation/index.md`.

## Verification

`cd website && npm run build`. The site already sets `onBrokenLinks: 'throw'` and
`onBrokenMarkdownLinks: 'throw'`, so the build **is** the acceptance gate: any inbound link still
pointing at a deleted `index.md`, or any markdown breakage, fails it. This runs in CI today as the
`build (docusaurus)` check â€” no new tooling is introduced. A successful build with the two sections
rendering their generated index (intro prose + auto-listed cards with blurbs, in the preserved
order) is the pass condition.

Confirmed there are no content inbound links to the two index pages beyond the navbar (only the
historical docs-site spec mentions them, which needs no change).

## Resulting anti-drift posture

Adding a how-to/explanation page = create the `.md` with its front-matter; it appears, once, in the
sidebar **and** the generated index, with its blurb, in position â€” no list to touch. Renaming or
moving a page carries its label and blurb with it. No central list can fall out of sync, and the
`onBrokenLinks: throw` build catches any dangling reference. The drift is designed out, not policed.
